However, simply creating a contract instance does not pose a security risk by itself; the risk depends on how the instance is used.\n- **Malware score?** 0 — no malicious behavior is indicated.\n- **Obfuscated?** 0 — code is clear and autogenerated.\n- **Scores adjustment?** Given the analysis, the minimal security risk score of 0.1 seems unwarranted. The code is purely boilerplate for contract interaction, with no embedded logic or data handling that could lead to security issues.\n\n---\n\n### **Summary & Final Assessment:**\n\n- **Malware score:** All reports correctly identify no malicious code, so **0** is appropriate.\n- **Obfuscated score:** All are straightforward, autogenerated code, so **0**.\n- **Risk score:** Since the code is a benign factory for contract interaction with no dynamic behavior, no security risk exists; **0** is suitable.\n\n### **Overall Conclusion:**\n\n- The code is a standard, autogenerated ethers.js factory class for interacting with a known smart contract (Uniswap V2 Pair).
